response:
"the polynomial function f(x) = 2x³ - 3x² + ax + b has a root at x = 2, so f(2) = 0. substituting x = 2 into the equation gives 2(8) - 3(4) + 2a + b = 0 → 16 - 12 + 2a + b = 0 → 4 + 2a + b = 0. this is the first equation relating a and b. to find a second equation, we use the fact that x = 2 is a double root, meaning f(x) and f'(x) both equal zero at x = 2. the derivative f'(x) = 6x² - 6x + a. substituting x = 2 into f'(x) gives 6(4) - 6(2) + a = 24 - 12 + a = 12 + a = 0 → a = -12. substituting a = -12 into the first equation gives 4 + 2(-12) + b = 0 → 4 - 24 + b = 0 → b = 20. the values are a = -12 and b = 20."
